description:
You are an MLOps engineer who needs to demonstrate solid Linux-process-management skills while simulating a small ML experiment and archiving its artifacts.  
Carry out the following steps from the terminal.  The automated grader will look **only** at the final state of the filesystem, so be sure the required files are present and have the exact contents/format described.

1. Create an experiment root directory  
   /home/user/experiments/exp001  

2. Inside that directory, create a background “training” job.  
   • The job may be any command you like (a shell script, a small Python loop, etc.) as long as it:  
     – runs for a few seconds,  
     – prints at least the three epoch messages listed below to STDOUT **in this exact text** (one per line):  
       epoch 1/3 - loss: 0.90  
       epoch 2/3 - loss: 0.80  
       epoch 3/3 - loss: 0.70  
     – terminates normally with exit-code 0.  
   • Redirect that STDOUT into the file  
     /home/user/experiments/exp001/train.log  

3. While the job is running, capture its PID.  
   • You will later need this PID value, so store it somewhere temporary (an env-var, a temp file, etc.).  

4. When the process finishes, simulate creation of a model artifact by writing a small dummy file named  
   /home/user/experiments/exp001/artifacts/model.bin  
   The parent directory “artifacts” must be created by you.  
   The file model.bin must contain exactly a single text line:  
   dummy model binary data  

5. Finally, generate a JSON metadata file named  
   /home/user/experiments/exp001/experiment_metadata.json  
   with precisely this key order and formatting (no pretty printing, all on one line, use double quotes):  

   {"pid": <PID>, "exit_code": 0, "artifacts": ["artifacts/model.bin", "train.log"]}

   • Replace <PID> with the actual integer PID you captured.  
   • The value for "exit_code" must be 0.  
   • The artifacts list must be exactly as shown, in that order and with the exact relative paths.  

Verification details the grader will enforce:
• /home/user/experiments/exp001/train.log exists and contains at least the three epoch lines shown above in that exact text.  
• /home/user/experiments/exp001/artifacts/model.bin exists and its sole content is the line “dummy model binary data” followed by a single newline.  
• /home/user/experiments/exp001/experiment_metadata.json exists and matches all of:  
  – valid JSON,  
  – field "pid" is an integer > 0,  
  – field "exit_code" == 0,  
  – field "artifacts" is the 2-element array ["artifacts/model.bin", "train.log"].  

Nothing else will be graded, so you do not need to keep temporary files created along the way.
truth:
Directories that must exist after successful task completion:
- /home/user/experiments/exp001/
- /home/user/experiments/exp001/artifacts/

Files and their exact required contents:

1. /home/user/experiments/exp001/train.log  
   Exact (first) three lines, each terminated by a single newline character (additional lines are allowed but not required):  
   epoch 1/3 - loss: 0.90  
   epoch 2/3 - loss: 0.80  
   epoch 3/3 - loss: 0.70  

2. /home/user/experiments/exp001/artifacts/model.bin  
   Content (single line, terminated by newline):  
   dummy model binary data  

3. /home/user/experiments/exp001/experiment_metadata.json  
   Must be valid JSON.  The automated test will load the file and verify:  
   • json["pid"] is an int and >0  
   • json["exit_code"] == 0  
   • json["artifacts"] == ["artifacts/model.bin", "train.log"]

   Regex used by the grader (ignores whitespace):  
   ^\s*\{\s*"pid"\s*:\s*\d+\s*,\s*"exit_code"\s*:\s*0\s*,\s*"artifacts"\s*:\s*\[\s*"artifacts\/model\.bin"\s*,\s*"train\.log"\s*\]\s*\}\s*$

No other files are strictly required for verification.
